products.title: Iggy Oval Dining Table
products.description: An oval dining table carried on two slatted pedestal columns, so no one ever sits against a leg. The top is a single glued panel of solid oak, shaped until the ellipse reads as one calm line.
products.story: Iggy began as a question: how many chairs fit around a table with no corners? The answer, at the last count, was eleven — nine seated, two leaning in to argue.
details: Solid oak top, 40 mm, full-stave glued panel
Twin pedestal bases of vertical oak slats
Seats eight without touching a knee to wood
Finished with hardwax oil, repairable in place

products.title: Steve Coffee Table
products.description: A rectangular coffee table of uncompromising simplicity: a thick oak slab resting on two plank legs, joined so cleanly the eye keeps looking for the fixings. There are none to find.
products.story: Steve is the piece we hand apprentices first. Three boards, two joints, nowhere to hide. When their Steve is right, they are allowed near everything else.
details: Solid oak throughout, 38 mm boards
Plank legs housed in concealed sliding dovetails
Edges eased by hand, never routed
Grain matched across top and legs from one log

products.title: Duo Tiered Coffee Table
products.description: A two-level coffee table with an offset lower shelf — one plane for the life of the room, a second for the books and unfinished arguments that would otherwise cover it.
products.story: Duo exists because Astrid kept losing her reading glasses under the newspaper. The lower tier is precisely one folded broadsheet deep. That is not a coincidence.
details: Two solid oak planes, offset in overlap
Shelf carried on through-wedged battens
Underside finished as carefully as the top
Sized for sofas of ordinary ambition

products.title: Otto Pedestal Table
products.description: A round table on a single slatted column, sized for four people and one candle. The pedestal's vertical oak staves catch light like a curtain caught mid-fold.
products.story: Otto is the workshop's answer to small Copenhagen kitchens: a table with no legs to bark a shin on and no bad seat. The column is thirty-one staves; the thirty-second is on the wall, signed by whoever counted wrong.
details: Round solid oak top, 36 mm
Pedestal of vertical staves over a steel core
Weighted base plate hidden in the plinth
Comfortable for four, intimate for two

products.title: Rollo Dining Table
products.description: A massive oak dining table on four turned cylindrical legs, planted at the corners like it intends to stay for the century. The top is fifty millimetres of slow-dried oak with a hand-eased edge.
products.story: Rollo is named for the roll of the gouge that turns its legs — one long cut per leg, no sandpaper until the last minute. Knock a knuckle on the top and the whole table answers.
details: Solid oak top, 50 mm full-stave panel
Turned cylinder legs, wedged through the top
No aprons — knees go where they please
Ages to a deep honey patina under daily use

products.title: Rollo Coffee Table Set
products.description: The dining table's offspring: a nesting pair of low tables on the same turned legs, one long, one square. Together they hold a living room; apart they follow the conversation around it.
products.story: The set exists because offcuts of fifty-millimetre oak are too good to burn and too heavy to argue with. Two tables from one board — the grain runs from the long table straight into the square one.
details: Two tables: long and square, from one board
Turned legs matched to the Rollo dining table
Nest together to the footprint of the larger
Grain continues across both tops

products.title: Sam Side Table
products.description: A three-legged side table with a gently domed round top, standing steady on floors that have been settling since the last king but three. Sized for a lamp, a coffee, and one open book.
products.story: Sam is the smallest thing we make and the hardest to keep in stock. Three legs, because Copenhagen floors lean; a rounded top, because everything on a side table eventually gets knocked — better it lands on the rug.
details: Solid oak top with hand-rounded edge
Three splayed legs, wedged tenons
Tripod stance never rocks
Light enough to carry with one finger through a book
